evals.prompt: Set up the devops config for this project. Scan evals/files/aws-github-repo/ to detect the stack and generate .devops.yaml.
evals.expected_output: Detects AWS provider, us-east-2 region, s3 state backend, github-actions CI/CD, ECR registry, docker runtime. Shows an annotated .devops.yaml preview. Asks for confirmation before writing.
expectations: Shows a .devops.yaml preview annotated with source files
Does NOT write the file without explicit yes confirmation

evals.prompt: Generate .devops.yaml for this GCP project. Scan evals/files/gcp-gitlab-repo/ to detect what you can.
evals.expected_output: Detects GCP provider and gitlab-ci. Asks for cloud.region and other unknowns one question at a time. Shows preview after collecting answers.
expectations: Detects cloud.provider as google or gcp
Asks about unknown fields (region, account_alias, etc.) one at a time — not all at once
Does NOT write the file without explicit yes confirmation

evals.prompt:
Scan evals/files/aws-github-repo/ and generate the .devops.yaml. Pretend there is an existing .devops.yaml already in the project root with this content:

cloud:
  provider: aws
  region: us-east-1
  account_alias: oldname
terraform:
  state_backend: s3
  var_file: prod.tfvars

Show me what would change.
evals.expected_output: Detects that cloud.region should change from us-east-1 to us-east-2 and cloud.account_alias from oldname to acme. Shows a diff of changed fields only. Does not overwrite without confirmation.
expectations: Identifies cloud.region changing from us-east-1 to us-east-2
Shows only changed fields in the diff (unchanged fields omitted or noted as unchanged)
Does NOT write the file without explicit yes confirmation
